other_observers: Bryan Patrick; I found the bird. Others later saw it including John Drummond and his group of about 7 birders and Nancy Gobris, RMBO bird-bander saw it at about 3 P.M. the same day and in the same general area.
specific_location: Sherrie's or Small Headquarters Pond, Chico Basin Ranch.
description: A mostly yellow bird with a thin warbler-like bill, this distinctive bird is easy to identify. Its yellow spectacles stand out because they are bordered by a black cap and black below and behind the eye in what sort of looks like a thin black mask. From chin to undertail, the plumage was yellow on the ventral surface and dorsally it was an olive-green color. The bill was black and I didn't look at the leg color but you can if you look at the excellent photo below.Behaviors: This Kentucky Warbler spent the entire time foraging on the gound or up to one foot off the ground. It sometimes disappeared from view in 6-inch tall grasses. It stopped to pose for a few seconds now and again, but was mostly on the move.Call: none heardPlumage: alternate
